199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
283	
284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
287	
288	        if do_min_p:
289	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
290	
291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
292	        # Compute the probabilities.
293	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
294	        # Compute the log probabilities.
295	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
296	
297	        # Sample the next tokens.
298	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
299	            probs,
300	            logprobs,
301	            sampling_metadata,
302	            sampling_tensors,
303	            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
304	            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
305	        )
306	
307	        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
308	            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
309	            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
310	            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
311	            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
312	            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
313	        else:
314	            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
315	            # GPU-side tensors.
316	            on_device_tensors = None
317	
318	        # Get the logprobs query results.
319	        prompt_logprobs = None
320	        sample_logprobs = None
321	        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
322	            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
323	            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
324	                                  SampleResultArgsType)
325	            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
326	                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)
327	
328	        return _build_sampler_output(
329	            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
330	            sampling_metadata,
331	            prompt_logprobs,
332	            sample_logprobs,
333	            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
334	            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)

338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.

348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace

427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442	
443	
444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results

1646	                                      if scheduler_outputs.num_prefill_groups
1647	                                      > 0 else None),
1648	                    multi_modal_placeholders=(
1649	                        seq_group.multi_modal_placeholders
1650	                        if scheduler_outputs.num_prefill_groups > 0 else None),
1651	                    prompt_adapter_request=seq_group.prompt_adapter_request,
1652	                )
1653	            else:
1654	                # When SPMD mode is enabled, we only send delta data except for
1655	                # the first request to reduce serialization cost.
1656	                seq_data_delta = {}
1657	                for id, data in seq_data.items():
1658	                    seq_data_delta[id] = data.get_delta_and_reset()
1659	                seq_group_metadata = SequenceGroupMetadataDelta(
1660	                    seq_data_delta,
1661	                    seq_group.request_id,
1662	                    block_tables,
1663	                    is_prompt,
1664	                    do_sample=do_sample,
1665	                    token_chunk_size=token_chunk_size,
1666	                    computed_block_nums=common_computed_block_nums,
1667	                )
1668	            seq_group_metadata_list.append(seq_group_metadata)
1669	
1670	            if allow_async_output_proc:
1671	                allow_async_output_proc = self._allow_async_output_proc(
1672	                    seq_group)
1673	
1674	        # Now that the batch has been created, we can assume all blocks in the
1675	        # batch will have been computed before the next scheduling invocation.
1676	        # This is because the engine assumes that a failure in model execution
1677	        # will crash the vLLM instance / will not retry.
1678	        for scheduled_seq_group in scheduler_outputs.scheduled_seq_groups:
1679	            self.block_manager.mark_blocks_as_computed(
1680	                scheduled_seq_group.seq_group,
1681	                scheduled_seq_group.token_chunk_size)
1682	
1683	        self._seq_group_metadata_cache[self.next_cache_id].reset()
1684	
1685	        scheduler_time = time.perf_counter() - scheduler_start_time
1686	        # Add this to scheduler time to all the sequences that are currently
1687	        # running. This will help estimate if the scheduler is a significant
1688	        # component in the e2e latency.
1689	        for seq_group in self.running:
1690	            if seq_group is not None and seq_group.metrics is not None:
1691	                if seq_group.metrics.scheduler_time is not None:
1692	                    seq_group.metrics.scheduler_time += scheduler_time
1693	                else:
1694	                    seq_group.metrics.scheduler_time = scheduler_time
1695	
1696	        # Move to next cache (if exists)
1697	        self.cache_id = self.next_cache_id
1698	
1699	        # Return results
1700	        return (seq_group_metadata_list, scheduler_outputs,
1701	                allow_async_output_proc)

## Summary of vLLM Sampling Parameter Infrastructure

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase to understand how sampling parameters are defined, validated, serialized, and integrated into the HTTP API layer. Here's my detailed findings:

### 1. **SamplingParams Class Definition**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478751417-6onp1a/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Key Details:**
- Lines 118-122: `SamplingParams` is a `msgspec.Struct` with `omit_defaults=True` and `dict=True`, enabling efficient serialization via msgspec
- Lines 205-245: Field definitions include all sampling parameters
  - `top_p` (line 212): float, default 1.0
  - `top_k` (line 213): int, default 0
  - `min_p` (line 214): float, default 0.0
  - `temperature` (line 211): float, default 1.0
  - And many others (presence_penalty, frequency_penalty, repetition_penalty, etc.)

**Validation:**
- Lines 387-462: `_verify_args()` method contains all validation logic:
  - Line 417-418: `top_p` must be in (0, 1]
  - Line 420-425: `top_k` must be >= 0 or -1 (quietly accepts -1 as disabled, prefers 0), must be int
  - Line 426-428: `min_p` must be in [0, 1]
  - Line 378-382: When temperature < epsilon, top_p/top_k/min_p are reset (greedy sampling)

**Post-initialization Processing:**
- Lines 327-385: `__post_init__()` method handles:
  - best_of adjustment logic
  - temperature warning/clamping
  - Stop string normalization
  - Greedy sampling mode forcing

**Factory Method:**
- Lines 251-325: `from_optional()` static method creates SamplingParams with None handling and logit_bias clamping

### 2. **HTTP API Request Schema**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478751417-6onp1a/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

**ChatCompletionRequest (Lines 223-432):**
- Lines 258-275: Sampling parameter fields marked with comment blocks `--8<-- [start:chat-completion-sampling-params]`
  - Line 261: `top_k: Optional[int] = None`
  - Line 262: `min_p: Optional[float] = None`
  - Line 244-245: `temperature: Optional[float] = None` and `top_p: Optional[float] = None`
  
- Lines 426-432: `_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS` dict defines defaults:
  ```python
  _DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS: dict = {
      "repetition_penalty": 1.0,
      "temperature": 1.0,
      "top_p": 1.0,
      "top_k": 0,
      "min_p": 0.0,
  }
  ```

- Lines 452-525: `to_sampling_params()` method converts HTTP request to SamplingParams
  - Handles default value substitution
  - Processes guided_decoding, logit_bias, allowed_token_ids
  - Applies logits processor pattern validation

**CompletionRequest (Lines 741-920):**
- Lines 764-779: Similar sampling parameter fields with same comment markers
- Lines 867-873: Identical default sampling params

### 3. **Serialization/Deserialization**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478751417-6onp1a/vllm/executor/msgspec_utils.py`

**Custom Hooks:**
- Lines 10-19: `encode_hook()` - handles array serialization by converting to bytes
- Lines 22-30: `decode_hook()` - handles array deserialization

**Usage Pattern:**
- File `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478751417-6onp1a/tests/core/test_serialization.py` shows:
  - msgspec.msgpack.Encoder with `enc_hook=encode_hook`
  - msgspec.msgpack.Decoder with `dec_hook=decode_hook`
  - SamplingParams inherits from msgspec.Struct for automatic serialization

### 4. **Sampling Metadata and GPU Execution**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478751417-6onp1a/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**SamplingTensors Class (Lines 372-597):**
- Lines 376-384: Tensor definitions:
  ```python
  temperatures: torch.Tensor
  top_ps: torch.Tensor
  top_ks: torch.Tensor
  min_ps: torch.Tensor
  presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
  frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
  repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
  ```

- Lines 386-495: `from_sampling_metadata()` extracts parameters from SamplingParams:
  - Lines 415-420: Extracts top_p, min_p, top_k from sampling_params
  - Lines 426-430: Sets `do_top_p_top_k` and `do_min_p` flags based on parameter values
  - Lines 437-460: Builds batched tensors of parameters for GPU

- Lines 539-580: Converts Python lists to GPU tensors with pinning

**CUDA Integration:**
- File `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478751417-6onp1a/csrc/sampler.cu` (only 87 lines, contains `apply_repetition_penalties_kernel`)
- The actual top-k, top-p, min_p sampling logic is likely in flashinfer or another sampler backend

### 5. **API Request Flow**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478751417-6onp1a/vllm/entrypoints/openai/serving_completion.py`

**Conversion Pipeline (Lines 170-180):**
```python
if request.use_beam_search:
    sampling_params = request.to_beam_search_params(
        max_tokens, self.default_sampling_params)
else:
    sampling_params = request.to_sampling_params(
        max_tokens, self.model_config.logits_processor_pattern,
        self.default_sampling_params)
```

### 6. **V1 API Integration**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478751417-6onp1a/vllm/v1/request.py`

- Lines 30, 43: V1 Request class accepts `SamplingParams` from the same module
- Same SamplingParams class is reused across both V0 and V1 APIs

### 7. **Key Validation Constraints**

From `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775478751417-6onp1a/vllm/sampling_params.py` lines 387-462:

| Parameter | Constraint | Notes |
|-----------|-----------|-------|
| `top_p` | (0, 1] | Strict bounds check on line 417 |
| `top_k` | 0 or >= 1, or -1 | Integer type required (line 423-425); -1 is quietly accepted as disabled |
| `min_p` | [0, 1] | Inclusive bounds (line 426-428) |
| `temperature` | >= 0 | No upper bound in main validation |
| `presence_penalty` | [-2, 2] | Bounds on line 404-406 |
| `frequency_penalty` | [-2, 2] | Bounds on line 407-409 |
| `repetition_penalty` | > 0 | Must be strictly positive (line 410-413) |

### 8. **Config/Schema References**

The main configuration comes from:
- Protocol definitions in `/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`
- SamplingParams docstring in `/vllm/sampling_params.py` (lines 123-203) which follows OpenAI API spec
- Default values hardcoded in request classes

**Next Steps for Adding "Top-A":**

To add a new "Top-A" parameter, you would need to:
1. Add field to `SamplingParams` (line ~214, after `min_p`)
2. Add validation in `_verify_args()` method (line ~426-428)
3. Add field to `ChatCompletionRequest` and `CompletionRequest` (lines 262, 767)
4. Add to `_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS` dict (line ~431, 872)
5. Update `to_sampling_params()` to pass through the value (line ~474-476)
6. Add tensor extraction in `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` (line ~415-420)
7. Create tensor in `SamplingTensors.from_lists()` (line ~550)
8. Integrate into actual sampler/GPU kernel logic

#### `_apply_min_p()` Implementation (Lines 427-441)

```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from text-generation-webui
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### 7. **Top-K/Top-P Comparison - Model for Top-A**
**Lines 398-421: `_apply_top_k_top_p()`**

Shows the pattern to follow:
- Takes logits and parameter tensors
- Uses sorting and masking to filter tokens
- Returns modified logits
- This is the exact same pattern min_p follows

### **Data Flow Summary Diagram**

```
SamplingParams (high-level API)
    ↓
SequenceGroup (in engine)
    ↓
SequenceGroupMetadata (created by scheduler)
    ↓
SamplingMetadata.prepare() 
    ↓
SequenceGroupToSample (intermediate)
    ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()

    └─ Create tensor: min_ps_t = torch.tensor(...) (lines 551-556)
    ↓
SamplingTensors object with min_ps tensor
    ↓
Sampler.forward()
    └─ if do_min_p: _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
       └─ Modifies logits by masking low-probability tokens
    ↓
Output logits → softmax → probabilities → sampling
```

---

### **Key Insights for Implementing Top-A**

1. **Add to SamplingParams** (sampling_params.py, line ~214):
   ```python
   top_a: float = 0.0  # Add after min_p
   ```

2. **Validation** (sampling_params.py, _verify_args):
   ```python
   if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0:
       raise ValueError(f"top_a must be in [0, 1], got {self.top_a}.")
   ```

3. **Extract in SamplingTensors** (sampling_metadata.py, line ~416):
   ```python
   top_a = sampling_params.top_a
   ```

4. **Create tensor list** (sampling_metadata.py, lines ~444-446, 456-457):
   ```python
   top_as += [top_a] * prefill_len
   top_as += [top_a] * sample_lens
   ```

5. **Convert to tensor** (sampling_metadata.py, ~551-556):
   ```python
   top_as_t = torch.tensor(top_as, device="cpu", dtype=dtype, pin_memory=pin_memory)
   ```

6. **Add to SamplingTensors class** (sampling_metadata.py, line ~379):
   ```python
   top_as: torch.Tensor
   ```

7. **Return in SamplingTensors** (sampling_metadata.py, line ~588):
   ```python
   top_as=top_as_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
   ```

8. **Apply in Sampler** (sampler.py, after line 289):
   ```python
   if do_top_a:  # Conditional flag
       logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
   ```

9. **Implement _apply_top_a** (sampler.py, after _apply_min_p):
   ```python
   def _apply_top_a(logits: torch.Tensor, top_a: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
       probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
       top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
       # Top-A: keep tokens with cumulative probability >= top_a * max_prob
       sorted_probs, sorted_indices = torch.sort(probs, descending=True)
       cum_probs = torch.cumsum(sorted_probs, dim=-1)
       # ... mask logic similar to top_p ...
   ```

---

### **Files to Modify**
1. `/vllm/sampling_params.py` - Add parameter definition
2. `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` - Extract and convert to tensor
3. `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - Apply the filter
4. `/tests/samplers/test_sampler.py` - Add test cases

#### `_apply_min_p()` Implementation (Lines 427-441)

```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from text-generation-webui
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

- Returns modified logits
- This is the exact same pattern min_p follows

#### **V1 Sampler (Current) - `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`**

### **3. ORDER OF FILTER APPLICATION**

#### **V0 Sampler (vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py, Lines 269-295)**

**Sequential order in `forward()` method:**

1. **Line 269:** `_apply_min_tokens_penalty()` - Penalizes stop tokens if min_tokens not reached
2. **Lines 272-277:** `apply_penalties()` - Presence, frequency, repetition penalties
3. **Lines 281-282:** Temperature scaling (logits / temperature)
4. **Lines 284-286:** Top-k & top-p filtering (if needed)

6. **Line 293:** Softmax to probabilities
7. **Line 295:** Log-softmax to logprobs
8. **Lines 298-305:** Actual sampling (multinomial or greedy)

**Critical Finding:** Min-p is applied **AFTER** top-k/top-p filtering, not before!

#### **V1 Sampler (vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py, Lines 25-77)**

**Sequential order in `forward()` method:**

2. **Lines 44-45:** Apply bad words

### **4. MIN_P IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS**

#### **V0 Implementation (vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py, Lines 427-441)**

```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Key Points:**
- Takes **logits** as input (not probabilities)
- Computes softmax internally to get probabilities
- Finds max probability per sequence
- Scales min_p by max_prob: `scaled_min_p = min_p * max_prob`
- Masks tokens with `prob < scaled_min_p` to `-inf`
- Per-request parameter (1D tensor of shape `(batch_size,)`)

#### **V1 LogitsProcessor Implementation (vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py, Lines 225-306)**

```python
class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.min_p_count:
            return logits
        
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

**Key Differences from V0:**
- Structured as a separate `LogitsProcessor` class (modular design)
- Maintains internal state: `min_p_count`, `min_p_cpu_tensor`, `min_p_device`
- Tracks which requests have min_p > 0
- Pre-allocates tensors on CPU and device for efficiency
- Has `update_state()` method to handle batch updates (adds/removes/moves requests)
- Property `is_argmax_invariant()` returns `True` (min-p doesn't affect greedy sampling)

**Constructor (Lines 227-242):**
```python
def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool, device: DeviceLikeType):
    self.min_p_count: int = 0
    self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                        dtype=torch.float32,
                                        device="cpu",
                                        pin_memory=pin_memory)
    self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
    self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                  dtype=torch.float32,
                                                  device=device)
    self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
```

#### **TPU Implementation (vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py, Lines 125-145)**

```python
def apply_min_p(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
    valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
    logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Note:** Uses `~valid_token_mask` (inverted logic) for XLA compatibility on TPU.

---

### **5. PER-REQUEST PARAMETER MANAGEMENT**

#### **V0 Pattern (How batch of per-request values is created)**

**File:** `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`, Lines 407-461

```python
for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
    seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
    min_p = sampling_params.min_p  # Extract per-request parameter
    
    # ... check if needed
    if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
        do_min_p = True
    
    # Append for each token that needs sampling
    if seq_group.do_sample:
        sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
        min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens  # Replicate for all sample indices
```

**Result:** A flat list of min_p values, one per token position across entire batch.

#### **V1 LogitsProcessor Pattern (How updates are managed)**

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`, Lines 251-287

```python
def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
    if not batch_update:
        return
    
    # Process added requests
    for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
        min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
        if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
            needs_update = True
            self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
        if min_p:
            self.min_p_count += 1
    
    # Process removed/moved requests (with sophisticated tracking)
    # ...
    
    # Update device tensors
    if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
        self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
        self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
        self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
```

**Key Feature:** Tracks which requests have min_p > 0 and only updates/applies when needed.

#### **TPU Pattern (How values are populated)**

**File:** `vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py`

```python
self.min_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.min_p

if sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
    self.min_p_reqs.add(req_id)
```

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py`, Lines 105-123

```python
fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor, DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])

return cls(
    min_p=input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(xla_device),
    # ...
)
```

---

### **6. CUDA/TRITON KERNELS**

**No dedicated CUDA/Triton kernels for min_p filtering.** 

Implementations use:
- **Pure PyTorch**: `softmax()`, `amax()`, `masked_fill_()`
- **FlashInfer integration** (for top-k/top-p in V1): Only supports top-k and top-p, not min-p
- **TPU/XLA**: Custom implementations for XLA compatibility

---

### **7. EXAMPLE: How top_k and top_p Filter Order (Pattern for Top-A)**

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`, Lines 168-207

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
    
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k: Keep only top-k largest logits
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p: Keep only tokens in top probability mass p
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # At least one token
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Restore original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Pattern:** Both top-k and top-p operate on sorted logits, then scatter back to original order.

---

### **SUMMARY TABLE: Min-P Threading Through the Pipeline**

| Component | File | Line(s) | What Happens |
|-----------|------|---------|--------------|
| **Parameter Definition** | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 214, 261, 426-428 | Field declared, validated (0-1 range) |
| **V0 Metadata Building** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 416, 429, 446, 457 | Extracted from SamplingParams, check if needed, replicated per-token |
| **V0 Tensor Construction** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 551-556 | Converted to torch tensor with pinned memory |
| **V0 Filtering** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 288-289 | Applied as `_apply_min_p()` after top-k/top-p |
| **V1 LogitsProcessor Init** | `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | 227-242 | Pre-allocated CPU/device tensors |
| **V1 State Update** | `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | 251-287 | Batch updates tracked, device tensor synchronized |
| **V1 Filtering** | `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | 290-306 | Applied in argmax-invariant processor phase |
| **TPU Metadata** | `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` | 29, 105-123 | Exposed as tensor field, padded for XLA |
| **TPU Filtering** | `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` | 62-64, 125-145 | Applied early in sample() pipeline |

---

### **IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST FOR TOP-A**

1. **Add to SamplingParams** (`vllm/sampling_params.py`)
   - Add field: `top_a: float = 0.0` (line ~214)
   - Add validation: `0.0 <= top_a <= 1.0` (line ~426)

3. **V1 LogitsProcessor Implementation**

   - Add to `init_builtin_logitsprocs()` (line 488)
   - Determine if argmax_invariant (likely `True`, like min-p)

4. **TPU Implementation**
   - Add to `TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata` (line ~30)
   - Add to `DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS` (line ~12)
   - Implement `apply_top_a()` method in TPU sampler (lines 62-76)

5. **Algorithm Implementation**
   - Top-A filters based on absolute probability threshold relative to max
   - Similar to min-p but uses `max_prob * top_a` threshold instead

All key files and line numbers are provided above for direct reference during implementation!

```python
    top_p: torch.Tensor = None
    all_greedy: bool = True
    # ... other fields
```

**Default sampling params (Lines 10-19):**
```python
DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = dict(
    temperature=-1.0,
    min_p=0.0,
    top_k=0,
    top_p=1.0,
)
```

---

### **3. ORDER OF FILTER APPLICATION**

#### **V0 Sampler (vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py, Lines 269-295)**

**Sequential order in `forward()` method:**

1. **Line 269:** `_apply_min_tokens_penalty()` - Penalizes stop tokens if min_tokens not reached
2. **Lines 272-277:** `apply_penalties()` - Presence, frequency, repetition penalties
3. **Lines 281-282:** Temperature scaling (logits / temperature)
4. **Lines 284-286:** Top-k & top-p filtering (if needed)
5. **Lines 288-289:** **Min-p filtering** (AFTER top-k/top-p!)
6. **Line 293:** Softmax to probabilities
7. **Line 295:** Log-softmax to logprobs
8. **Lines 298-305:** Actual sampling (multinomial or greedy)

**Critical Finding:** Min-p is applied **AFTER** top-k/top-p filtering, not before!

#### **V1 Sampler (vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py, Lines 25-77)**

**Sequential order in `forward()` method:**

1. **Lines 42-43:** Apply allowed token IDs
2. **Lines 44-45:** Apply bad words
3. **Lines 48-49:** Apply non-argmax-invariant logits processors
4. **Lines 51-52:** Apply penalties (note: these are different from V0)
5. **Lines 113-118:** Apply temperature
6. **Lines 117-118:** Apply argmax-invariant logits processors (includes min-p!)
7. **Lines 121-126:** TopK/TopP sampling via FlashInfer/native

#### **TPU Sampler (vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py, Lines 62-76)**

**Sequential order in `sample()` method:**

1. **Line 60:** Apply temperature
2. **Lines 63-64:** Apply min_p (if present)
3. **Lines 67-72:** Apply top_k and top_p

---

### **4. MIN_P IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS**

#### **V0 Implementation (vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py, Lines 427-441)**

```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Key Points:**
- Takes **logits** as input (not probabilities)
- Computes softmax internally to get probabilities
- Finds max probability per sequence
- Scales min_p by max_prob: `scaled_min_p = min_p * max_prob`
- Masks tokens with `prob < scaled_min_p` to `-inf`
- Per-request parameter (1D tensor of shape `(batch_size,)`)

#### **V1 LogitsProcessor Implementation (vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py, Lines 225-306)**

```python
class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.min_p_count:
            return logits
        
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

**Key Differences from V0:**
- Structured as a separate `LogitsProcessor` class (modular design)
- Maintains internal state: `min_p_count`, `min_p_cpu_tensor`, `min_p_device`
- Tracks which requests have min_p > 0
- Pre-allocates tensors on CPU and device for efficiency
- Has `update_state()` method to handle batch updates (adds/removes/moves requests)
- Property `is_argmax_invariant()` returns `True` (min-p doesn't affect greedy sampling)

**Constructor (Lines 227-242):**
```python
def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool, device: DeviceLikeType):
    self.min_p_count: int = 0
    self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                        dtype=torch.float32,
                                        device="cpu",
                                        pin_memory=pin_memory)
    self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
    self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                  dtype=torch.float32,
                                                  device=device)
    self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
```

#### **TPU Implementation (vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py, Lines 125-145)**

```python
def apply_min_p(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
    valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
    logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Note:** Uses `~valid_token_mask` (inverted logic) for XLA compatibility on TPU.

---

### **5. PER-REQUEST PARAMETER MANAGEMENT**

#### **V0 Pattern (How batch of per-request values is created)**

**File:** `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`, Lines 407-461

```python
for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
    seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
    min_p = sampling_params.min_p  # Extract per-request parameter
    
    # ... check if needed
    if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
        do_min_p = True
    
    # Append for each token that needs sampling
    if seq_group.do_sample:
        sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
        min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens  # Replicate for all sample indices
```

**Result:** A flat list of min_p values, one per token position across entire batch.

#### **V1 LogitsProcessor Pattern (How updates are managed)**

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`, Lines 251-287

```python
def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
    if not batch_update:
        return
    
    # Process added requests
    for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
        min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
        if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
            needs_update = True
            self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
        if min_p:
            self.min_p_count += 1
    
    # Process removed/moved requests (with sophisticated tracking)
    # ...
    
    # Update device tensors
    if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
        self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
        self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
        self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
```

**Key Feature:** Tracks which requests have min_p > 0 and only updates/applies when needed.

#### **TPU Pattern (How values are populated)**

**File:** `vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py`

```python
self.min_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.min_p

if sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
    self.min_p_reqs.add(req_id)
```

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py`, Lines 105-123

```python
fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor, DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])

return cls(
    min_p=input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(xla_device),
    # ...
)
```

---

### **6. CUDA/TRITON KERNELS**

**No dedicated CUDA/Triton kernels for min_p filtering.** 

Implementations use:
- **Pure PyTorch**: `softmax()`, `amax()`, `masked_fill_()`
- **FlashInfer integration** (for top-k/top-p in V1): Only supports top-k and top-p, not min-p
- **TPU/XLA**: Custom implementations for XLA compatibility

---

### **7. EXAMPLE: How top_k and top_p Filter Order (Pattern for Top-A)**

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`, Lines 168-207

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
    
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k: Keep only top-k largest logits
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p: Keep only tokens in top probability mass p
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # At least one token
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Restore original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Pattern:** Both top-k and top-p operate on sorted logits, then scatter back to original order.

---

### **SUMMARY TABLE: Min-P Threading Through the Pipeline**

| Component | File | Line(s) | What Happens |
|-----------|------|---------|--------------|
| **Parameter Definition** | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 214, 261, 426-428 | Field declared, validated (0-1 range) |
| **V0 Metadata Building** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 416, 429, 446, 457 | Extracted from SamplingParams, check if needed, replicated per-token |
| **V0 Tensor Construction** | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 551-556 | Converted to torch tensor with pinned memory |
| **V0 Filtering** | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 288-289 | Applied as `_apply_min_p()` after top-k/top-p |
| **V1 LogitsProcessor Init** | `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | 227-242 | Pre-allocated CPU/device tensors |
| **V1 State Update** | `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | 251-287 | Batch updates tracked, device tensor synchronized |
| **V1 Filtering** | `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | 290-306 | Applied in argmax-invariant processor phase |
| **TPU Metadata** | `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` | 29, 105-123 | Exposed as tensor field, padded for XLA |
| **TPU Filtering** | `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` | 62-64, 125-145 | Applied early in sample() pipeline |

---

### **IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST FOR TOP-A**

1. **Add to SamplingParams** (`vllm/sampling_params.py`)
   - Add field: `top_a: float = 0.0` (line ~214)
   - Add validation: `0.0 <= top_a <= 1.0` (line ~426)

3. **V1 LogitsProcessor Implementation**

4. **TPU Implementation**

5. **Algorithm Implementation**

@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],
        top_ks: list[int],
        min_ps: list[float],
        presence_penalties: list[float],
        frequency_penalties: list[float],
        repetition_penalties: list[float],
        prompt_tokens: list[array],
        output_tokens: list[array],
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
        # pinned memory.
        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()

        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens

        if do_penalties:
            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                prompt_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                output_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
        else:
            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
            prompt_t = empty_tensor
            output_t = empty_tensor

        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
            temperatures,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            min_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            presence_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            frequency_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            repetition_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ks,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
        # transfer to device.

        return cls(
            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                       non_blocking=True),
            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                         non_blocking=True),
            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                           non_blocking=True),
            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        )

204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
283	
284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
287	
288	        if do_min_p:
289	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
290	
291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
292	        # Compute the probabilities.
293	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
294	        # Compute the log probabilities.
295	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
296	
297	        # Sample the next tokens.
298	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
299	            probs,
300	            logprobs,
301	            sampling_metadata,
302	            sampling_tensors,
303	            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
304	            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
305	        )
306	
307	        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
308	            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
309	            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
310	            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
311	            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
312	            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
313	        else:
314	            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
315	            # GPU-side tensors.
316	            on_device_tensors = None
317	
318	        # Get the logprobs query results.
319	        prompt_logprobs = None

self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ):
        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
        decodes of the same sequences.
        """
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
        # have pinned memory.
        self._sampling_tensors = None

        # Initialize new sampling tensors
        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)

        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
        self._do_min_p = do_min_p

    def forward(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
        """
        Single-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor

        Multi-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
            tensor
            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
            in the
            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
            structure

        Args:
            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
        """
        assert logits is not None
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
        elif self._do_penalties:
            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
            # between decode runs.
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)

        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
        do_min_p = self._do_min_p

        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)

        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
        if do_penalties:
            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)

        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))

        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)

        if do_min_p:
            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)

        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
        # Compute the probabilities.
        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
        # Compute the log probabilities.
        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

        # Sample the next tokens.
        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
            probs,
            logprobs,
            sampling_metadata,
            sampling_tensors,
            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
        )

        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
        else:
            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
            # GPU-side tensors.
            on_device_tensors = None

        # Get the logprobs query results.
        prompt_logprobs = None

def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits


def _greedy_sample(
    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
    samples: torch.Tensor,
) -> SampleResultType:
    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.

    Args:
        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
            seq_group.do_sample is False.
    Returns:
        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
    """
    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
    sample_idx = 0
    results: SampleResultType = []
    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
        if not seq_group.do_sample:
            results.append(([], []))
            continue

        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)

###### ----- Built-in LogitsProcessor impls below here


class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.min_p_count: int = 0

        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        # Pre-allocated device tensor
        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
                                                      device=device)
        # Current slice of the device tensor
        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
        return True

    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return

        needs_update = False
        # Process added requests.
        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
                needs_update = True
                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
            if min_p:
                self.min_p_count += 1

        if self.min_p_count:
            # Process removed requests.
            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
            for index in batch_update.removed:
                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
                    self.min_p_count -= 1

            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
                change = (min_p_a :=
                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
                needs_update |= change
                if change:
                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b

        # Update tensors if needed.
        size = batch_update.batch_size
        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.min_p_count:
            return logits

        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        # Adjust min_p
        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits


class LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
        super().__init__()
        self.biases: dict[int, dict[int, float]] = {}
        self.device = device
        self.pin_memory = pin_memory

        self.bias_tensor: torch.Tensor = torch.tensor(())
        self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor([], torch.int32),
                             self._device_tensor([], torch.int32))

for index in to_remove:
                    del self.min_toks[index]

        # Update tensors if needed.
        if needs_update:
            reqs: list[int] = []
            tok_ids: list[int] = []
            for req, (_, _, stop_tok_ids) in self.min_toks.items():
                reqs.extend([req] * len(stop_tok_ids))
                tok_ids.extend(stop_tok_ids)

            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))

    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
        return (torch.tensor(data,
                             device="cpu",
                             dtype=dtype,
                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
                                                            non_blocking=True))

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if self.min_toks:
            # Inhibit EOS token for requests which have not reached min length
            logits[self.logits_slice] = -float("inf")
        return logits


def init_builtin_logitsprocs(pin_memory_available: bool, max_num_reqs: int,
                             device: torch.device) -> LogitsProcessorManager:
    """Construct 'builtin' vLLM logitsprocs which the engine
    loads by default.

    Args:
      pin_memory_available: pinned memory is available for use
                            for use by logitsproc
      max_num_reqs: ceiling on request count in persistent batch
      device: inference device

    Returns:
      Data structure encapsulating loaded logitsprocs
    """
    min_tokens_logitproc = MinTokensLogitsProcessor(
        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
    logit_bias_logitproc = LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(
        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
    min_p_logitproc = MinPLogitsProcessor(
        pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
        device=device,
        # +1 for temporary swap space
        max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
    return LogitsProcessorManager(
        non_argmax_invariant=[
            min_tokens_logitproc,
            logit_bias_logitproc,
        ],
        argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc],
    )

115

118	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
119	
120	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
121	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
122

127	        logits: torch.Tensor,

129	    ) -> torch.Tensor:

132	        """
133	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
134	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
135	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
136	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
137	                                       dim=-1,
138	                                       keepdim=True)
139	        # Reshape min_p for broadcasting
140	        adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
141	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
142	        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
143	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)

greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)

        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None

        # Apply temperature.
        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)

        # Apply min_p.
        if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
            logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)

        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
            logits,
            sampling_metadata.generators,
            sampling_metadata.top_k,
            sampling_metadata.top_p,
        )

        sampled = torch.where(sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
                              greedy_sampled, random_sampled)
        return sampled

    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)

    def gather_logprobs(
        self,
        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
        num_logprobs: int,
        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
        """
        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.

        Args:
          logits: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
                        retain per token
          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
                     with (num tokens) elements

        Returns:
          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
        """
        # Find the topK values.
        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
                                                 num_logprobs,
                                                 dim=-1)

        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)

        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)

        # Concatenate together with the topk.
        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)

        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)

        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)

    def apply_min_p(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        min_p: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Filters logits using adaptive probability thresholding.
        """
        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        # Reshape min_p for broadcasting
        adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)
        logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
        return logits

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

import torch

from vllm.v1.worker.tpu_input_batch import InputBatch

DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = dict(
    temperature=-1.0,
    min_p=0.0,
    # strictly disabled for now
    top_k=0,
    top_p=1.0,
    # frequency_penalties=0.0,
    # presence_penalties=0.0,
    # repetition_penalties=0.0,
)


@dataclass
class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
    # This class exposes a more xla-friendly interface than SamplingMetadata
    # on TPU, in particular all arguments should be traceable and no optionals
    # are allowed, to avoid graph recompilation on Nones.
    temperature: torch.Tensor = None

    min_p: torch.Tensor = None
    top_k: torch.Tensor = None
    top_p: torch.Tensor = None

    all_greedy: bool = True

    # Whether logprobs are to be gathered in this batch of request. To balance
    # out compile time and runtime, a fixed `max_number_logprobs` value is used
    # when gathering logprobs, regardless of the values specified in the batch.
    logprobs: bool = False

    # TODO No penalties for now
    no_penalties: bool = True
    prompt_token_ids = None
    frequency_penalties = None
    presence_penalties = None
    repetition_penalties = None
    # should use tensor
    output_token_ids: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: list())

    min_tokens = None  # impl is not vectorized

    logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int, float]]] = field(
        default_factory=lambda: list())

    allowed_token_ids_mask = None
    bad_words_token_ids = None

    # Generator not supported by xla
    _generators: dict[int,
                      torch.Generator] = field(default_factory=lambda: dict())

    @property
    def generators(self) -> dict[int, torch.Generator]:
        # Generator not supported by torch/xla. This field must be immutable.
        return self._generators

    @classmethod
    def from_input_batch(
        cls,
        input_batch: InputBatch,
        padded_num_reqs: int,
        xla_device: torch.device,
        generate_params_if_all_greedy: bool = False
    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
        """
        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.

        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.

        Args:
            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
            xla_device: The XLA device.
            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
        """
        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)

        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs

        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
            # Pad value is the default one.
            cpu_tensor[num_reqs:padded_num_reqs] = fill_val

        fill_slice(input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_k"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])

        # Slice persistent device tensors to a fixed pre-compiled padded shape.
        return cls(
            temperature=input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].
            to(xla_device),
            all_greedy=input_batch.all_greedy,
            # TODO enable more and avoid returning None values
            top_p=input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            top_k=input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            min_p=input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            logprobs=needs_logprobs)
